rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly to frame a central theoretical backdrop: Airbnb competes with hotels, with stronger substitution in lower-end segments. The citing paper does not just list it in background; it explicitly uses this prior finding to motivate hypotheses about where demand should shift after Uber/Lyft exits and to interpret hotel-side heterogeneity results. In the contribution and conclusion sections, the authors state they reconfirm and build on this Airbnb-hotel competition pattern while introducing ride-sharing availability as a key moderator.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Zervas et al. (2017)'s Airbnb-versus-hotel competition line by adding exogenous variation in ride-sharing availability as a moderator of substitution. It shows that Uber/Lyft exit shifts demand away from low-end Airbnb properties toward low-end hotels, preserving and elaborating the segment-level competition pattern.
